title: Berchtold Chromophare Surgical Light – Light Head Power Supply Failure
description: Guide for troubleshooting light head power supply failures on Berchtold Chromophare surgical lights, focusing on external causes and safe escalation.

steps.title: Verify Main Power Supply
steps.instructions: Ensure the surgical light is connected to a live power source. Check circuit breakers and any outlet switches. Why it matters: A simple power interruption is the most common cause of light failure.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cables and Connections
steps.instructions: Check the power cable for visible damage, kinks, or loose connections. Confirm that the connectors to the light head and control panel are secure. Why it matters: Faulty cables or loose plugs can cause intermittent or total power loss.

steps.title: Check Control Console/Interface
steps.instructions: Verify that the main control console or wall switch is operational. Attempt to cycle power on/off to reset the system. Why it matters: Some Chromophare models have safety interlocks that prevent light activation if the control console detects errors.

steps.title: Inspect Light Head for Obvious Issues
steps.instructions: Look for burned-out LED modules or unusual discoloration. Listen for abnormal sounds from the internal fan or power module. Why it matters: Some external symptoms may indicate power supply stress or failure.

steps.title: Test Alternative Light Head (if available)
steps.instructions: Swap the problematic light head with a known working one on the same control system. Why it matters: Confirms whether the issue is with the light head or the power supply/control system.

steps.title: Check Fuses or Resettable Protection
steps.instructions: Some Chromophare series models have internal fuses or reset buttons accessible without disassembly. Why it matters: A blown fuse or tripped protection circuit can mimic full power failure.

CCR.Complaint: Light head will not power on; no illumination observed.
CCR.Cause: Checked power source, cables, control console, and connectors; all verified functional. Fault traced to internal power supply of light head.
CCR.Resolution: Light head removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for manufacturer repair.

helpfulDetails: Outlet tested: Yes
Cables swapped: Yes, no change
Indicator lights on console: Normal
Unusual sounds/heat: None
Final device status: Removed from service

title: Berchtold Chromophare Surgical Light – Control Panel or Wall Controller Malfunction
description: Guide for troubleshooting control panel or wall controller malfunctions on Berchtold Chromophare surgical lights, focusing on external checks and safe escalation.

steps.title: Verify Power to the System
steps.instructions: Ensure the surgical light and wall controller are receiving power. Check for tripped breakers, blown fuses, or unplugged connectors. Why: Lack of power is the most common external cause of unresponsive controls.

steps.title: Inspect Wall Controller Connections
steps.instructions: Confirm the wall controller is properly connected to the light system. Check for loose, damaged, or kinked cables. Why: Poor connections can prevent signals from reaching the light head.

steps.title: Check for Visible Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Examine the control panel and wall controller for cracks, liquid spills, or stuck buttons. Clean with manufacturer-approved wipes if necessary. Why: Debris or liquid ingress can interfere with electrical contacts.

steps.title: Test Button Functionality
steps.instructions: Press each button on the control panel and wall controller while observing the light response. Identify if specific functions are unresponsive or intermittent. Why: Helps isolate whether the issue is with specific buttons or the entire controller.

steps.title: Swap Controllers (if available)
steps.instructions: If a spare wall controller or control panel is available, swap it to see if the problem persists. Why: Confirms whether the issue lies with the controller or the light head/system.

steps.title: Check Indicator Lights or Error Codes
steps.instructions: Observe any status LEDs on the wall controller or light head. Reference manufacturer troubleshooting codes if present. Why: Indicator lights can provide diagnostic clues without opening the device.

CCR.Complaint: Wall controller buttons are unresponsive; light intensity cannot be adjusted.
CCR.Cause: Power verified, connections intact, no visible damage. Wall controller appears to be internally malfunctioning.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for manufacturer evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Power outlet checked and confirmed live
Cables inspected and reseated
Control panel buttons tested individually
Indicator LEDs monitored for error codes
Wall controller swap attempted if spare available
Final device status: Out of Service

title: Berchtold Chromophare Surgical Light – Arm Balance or Suspension Drift Issues
description: Guide for troubleshooting arm balance or suspension drift issues on Berchtold Chromophare surgical lights, focusing on external causes and safe escalation.

steps.title: Verify Power and Control Settings
steps.instructions: Ensure the light is plugged into a stable power source. Some models have electronically assisted suspension; verify the control panel or wall controller shows normal operation. Why: Power fluctuations or inactive motorized assists can affect suspension tension.

steps.title: Inspect Mechanical Locks and Brakes
steps.instructions: Check that all arm rotation locks and brake knobs are fully engaged and not loose. Tighten or adjust according to manufacturer specifications without disassembly. Why: Loose brakes can cause the arm to drift even if internal balance is correct.

steps.title: Check Arm Joints and Pivots
steps.instructions: Visually inspect joints for wear, corrosion, or debris. Move the arm through its full range and listen for unusual resistance or grinding. Why: Dirt or wear at pivot points can create uneven tension and drift.

steps.title: Assess Counterbalance Mechanisms
steps.instructions: Identify the spring, gas, or hydraulic balance mechanism (visible externally if possible). Ensure cables or springs are properly seated and not visibly stretched, kinked, or detached. Why: Improperly seated counterbalance components are a common cause of drift.

steps.title: Test Movement Under Load
steps.instructions: Gently move the light head up and down while observing for drift. If the light drifts under normal load, note the direction and speed. Why: Confirms whether the issue is balance-related or external obstruction.

steps.title: Check for Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Ensure the mounting surface is secure and level. Verify that no external equipment or cables are pulling on the arm. Why: External forces can mimic suspension failure.

CCR.Complaint: The surgical light arm drifts downward when positioned; it does not stay in place.
CCR.Cause: Observed loose brake knobs and slight misalignment of pivot points causing uneven suspension tension.
CCR.Resolution: Tightened all brake knobs and inspected pivot points. Noted that arm drift persists; light removed from service and labeled Out of Service for manufacturer evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Check which arm(s) are affected.
Note any unusual noise, roughness, or resistance when moving the arm.
Document whether drift occurs unloaded or under typical surgical load.
Record final device status (Out of Service, pending repair).

title: Berchtold Chromophare Surgical Light – Bulb or LED Module Failure
description: Guide for troubleshooting reduced or absent light output due to failed bulb or LED module on Berchtold Chromophare surgical lights, focusing on external causes before module replacement.

steps.title: Verify Power Supply
steps.instructions: Check that the surgical light is properly connected to a live power source. Confirm that the breaker or outlet is functioning using a multimeter or a known working device. Why it matters: No light output may be due to lack of power rather than bulb failure.

steps.title: Inspect Light Controls
steps.instructions: Test the wall controller and light head controls for proper response. Ensure intensity settings are at an appropriate level. Why it matters: A failed control may mimic a bulb or LED failure.

steps.title: Check Indicator Lights
steps.instructions: Some Chromophare lights have LED indicators that show operational status. Look for fault indicators or warning lights. Why it matters: This helps distinguish control issues from actual light source failure.

steps.title: Visual Inspection of the Light Head
steps.instructions: Look for obvious signs of bulb burn-out (filament break, darkened glass) or LED module discoloration. Inspect for physical damage, loose connectors, or overheating evidence. Why it matters: Confirms whether the light source itself is damaged.

steps.title: Swap or Test Bulb/LED Module (if possible)
steps.instructions: If spare bulbs or LED modules are available, replace with a known working unit. Test the light operation with the replacement. Why it matters: Confirms whether the failure is isolated to the bulb/module.

steps.title: Check for Loose or Damaged Connectors
steps.instructions: Ensure all connectors between the power supply and the light head are fully seated. Look for bent pins or corrosion. Why it matters: Electrical connection issues can mimic light source failure.

CCR.Complaint: Light output is flickering or completely out on surgical light head.
CCR.Cause: Visual inspection shows burned-out bulb or faulty LED module; power and controls verified functional.
CCR.Resolution: Removed failed bulb/module, labeled light Out of Service, escalated to manufacturer for replacement.

helpfulDetails: Outlet tested and verified
Power cable integrity
Control panel response
Indicator light status
Physical condition of the bulb/module
Any unusual heat or odor from the light head
Final device status: Out of Service or operational after module swap

title: Berchtold Chromophare Surgical Light – Internal Wiring Faults in Light Head or Arm
description: Guide for troubleshooting internal wiring faults in Berchtold Chromophare surgical lights, focusing on external checks before escalation and ensuring patient safety.

steps.title: Power Verification
steps.instructions: Confirm that the surgical light is plugged into a properly grounded, live outlet. Check that circuit breakers or fuses are not tripped or blown. Why: Many internal wiring issues are mimicked by simple power interruptions.

steps.title: Visual Inspection of External Wiring
steps.instructions: Inspect visible cables along the arm, suspension, and control panel for damage, fraying, or loose connections. Ensure strain reliefs are intact and there are no pinched wires. Why: External wiring faults can present similarly to internal faults and are easier to fix.

steps.title: Check Light Head and Arm Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure the connectors between the light head and arm are fully seated. Look for corrosion, bent pins, or discoloration. Why: Loose or corroded connections can cause intermittent faults that mimic internal wiring issues.

steps.title: Test Light Function Across Movement Range
steps.instructions: Slowly move the light through its full range of motion while observing for flicker, dropouts, or erratic movement. Listen for unusual sounds indicating wire contact issues inside the arm. Why: Faults that appear only in certain positions indicate internal wiring stress or break.

steps.title: Document Any Symptoms
steps.instructions: Note exactly when flickering or loss of power occurs (e.g., specific arm angles). Photograph any obvious damage or wear.

CCR.Complaint: Light flickers and loses power intermittently when moving the arm; sometimes the light fails to turn on.
CCR.Cause: Visual inspection revealed no external cable damage; intermittent failures occur at certain arm positions, indicating internal wiring stress or break.
CCR.Resolution: Unit removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for manufacturer repair or Clinical Engineering bench evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Outlet verified as live.
Arm movement tested; fault occurs at specific angles.
No unusual heat, odor, or visible cable damage externally.
Control panel and connectors tested and seated properly.
Final device status: Out of Service.
